communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: ALPR Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

This request is made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires, is anticipated.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

LPL

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Laconia School District/SAU30)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Laconia School District/SAU30)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The final report of the Under Secretary of Defense For Intelligence and Security following its review into Elon Musk's security clearance and his compliance with federal reporting protocols, as recently discussed in the New York Times:  https://www.nytimes.com/2024/12/17/technology/elon-musk-spacex-national-security-reporting.html

title: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of all reports, lists, databases, or individual documents created since 1/1/16 detail lawsuits brought against the city's police department. This should include any documentation mechanism your department uses to track lawsuits. It should also include any documents your department submits to local, state, or federal agencies listing the number and status of lawsuits brought against the police department. This request should include documents compiled quarterly, annually, or in other increments and documents or databases that contain responsive information over a longer period. 

- The records should include, if it exists, the following information: 
Settlement amounts
Case category (civil rights, collisions, etc) 
Case type/description (harassment, crowd control, in-custody death, etc)
Involved officers
Case numbers
Total expenses (this should include the legal costs to the city, including outsite litigation costs.) 

- Any corrective action reports that describe the incident that caused the lawsuit and the corrective actions taken to minimize risk, prevent recurrence, and hold the department and the involved officers accountable. The reports are often sent to respective bodies to approve settlements (city council, board of supervisors, controller, or risk management office) or created by another office within the city or county. 
- Copies of settlement agreements 

Only include settlements above $75,000 and lawsuits related to civil rights violations, collisions, professional negligence, and labor.

Search January 1, 2016 to today's date.

When possible, produce documents in spreadsheet format (excel, csv). California public records law requires the custodian of records to assist in finding records.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 days.

Sincerely,

J.
